title: Rough Crumbly British Bread Casserole
description: A traditional Yorkshire dish featuring double-rise bread and vegan white wine vinegar, perfect for a sick day. This casserole is bitter, crumbly, and comforting.

ingredients.name: double-rise bread
ingredients.notes: stale or day-old bread works best

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the double-rise bread into 1-inch cubes and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the vegan white wine vinegar, vegetable broth, nutritional yeast, salt, and black pepper.
Add the bread cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the vinegar mixture.
Add the cooked onion and mushroom mixture to the bowl and toss to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a scallop shell or a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Bake for 35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is heated through.
